commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=47567bb15bb7f35420bfc780656a9e21d6022a2a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds opt-in ACP image attachment forwarding for `sessions_spawn`, metadata-gated Gateway image handling, docs/config schema updates, focused tests, and a live ACP bind timeout test knob.

PR surface: Source +189, Tests +235, Docs +2. Total +426 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main rejects ACP runtime attachments before `spawnAcpDirect`, matching the linked report. I did not run the live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to resolve the dirty branch and decide whether to accept the new config gate and ACP image trust boundary; this is not a repair-only merge path.

target.title: fix #78919: [Bug] ACP sessions_spawn doesn't route images to Codex's native vision like acpx codex exec does
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP `sessions_spawn` did not route image attachments through the same native vision-capable path that Codex ACP can use, and an earlier PR head also carried unrelated shrinkwrap registry churn.
- Solution: Keep ACP image forwarding behind explicit attachment gates, require persisted ACP session metadata before Gateway skips the normal image-support check, remove the unrelated shrinkwrap rewrite, and allow the ACP bind live test request timeout to be increased for slower live providers.
- What changed: Updated ACP attachment forwarding/config docs/tests, tightened the Gateway ACP image bypass, narrowed one Bonjour test assertion exposed by the current main test typecheck, and added `OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` for live ACP bind proof runs.
- What did NOT change: No dependency manifests or shrinkwrap files are changed on the final rebased branch.

Fixes #78919

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` can accept image attachments, convert them to the Gateway base64 image attachment shape, and preserve them through Gateway attachment normalization, while Gateway no longer bypasses image capability checks for ACP-shaped session keys that do not have persisted ACP metadata.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v24.16.0, current rebased PR worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936` at `98d0decb1019672fef96f965253255bfa5254fe9`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
    env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936 \
    node --import tsx \
    /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-78936-evidence/acp-image-routing-proof-current.mjs

  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
    env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936 \
    pnpm exec vitest run \
      src/gateway/live-agent-probes.test.ts \
      src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts \
      src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts

  docker run --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    -v /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936:/repo \
    -v /home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0:/node24 \
    -v /usr/local/node:/usr/local/node \
    -v /home/0668001030/.codex:/root/.codex:rw \
    -w /repo \
    -e HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
    -e HTTP_PROXY=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_AGENT=codex \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_IMAGE_PROBE=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=360000 \
    openclaw-codex-proof:jammy \
    sh -lc 'export PATH="/node24/bin:/usr/local/node/bin:$PATH"; node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts'

  docker run --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    -v /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936:/repo \
    -v /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-78936-evidence:/evidence \
    -v /home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0:/node24 \
    -v /usr/local/node:/usr/local/node \
    -v /home/0668001030/.codex:/root/.codex:ro \
    -w /repo \
    -e HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
    -e HTTP_PROXY=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
    -e OPENAI_API_KEY \
    -e OPENCLAW_PROOF_WORKTREE=/repo \
    -e OPENCLAW_PROOF_EVIDENCE_DIR=/evidence \
    -e OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=98d0decb1019672fef96f965253255bfa5254fe9 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_CODEX_MODEL=gpt-5.5 \
    openclaw-codex-proof:jammy \
    sh -lc 'export PATH="/node24/bin:/usr/local/node/bin:$PATH"; node --import tsx /evidence/sessions-spawn-acp-image-live-proof-current.mjs 2>&1 | tee /evidence/sessions-spawn-acp-image-live-proof-current.log'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```json
  {
    "resolverStatus": "ok",
    "acpAttachment": {
      "mediaType": "image/png",
      "data": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
    },
    "gatewayAttachment": {
      "type": "image",
      "source": {
        "type": "base64",
        "media_type": "image/png",
        "data": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
      }
    },
    "normalizedAttachment": {
      "type": "image",
      "mimeType": "image/png",
      "content": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
    },
    "droppedByGatewayNormalizer": false
  }
  ```
  ```text
  [live-acp-bind] using Codex ACP model gpt-5.5
  [live-acp-bind] using parent live model openai/gpt-5.4
  [live-acp-bind] starting gateway on port 50000
  [live-acp-bind] gateway startup returned
  [live-acp-bind] gateway port is reachable
  [live-acp-bind] gateway websocket connected
  [live-acp-bind] bind command completed
  [live-acp-bind] binding announced for session agent:codex:acp:db16218c-23a6-48a4-9251-ed3cda7b69c4
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound follow-up token not observed yet; retrying
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 2)
  [live-acp-bind] bound follow-up token not observed yet; retrying
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 3)
  SKIP: codex ACP bind completed, but the bound session did not emit an assistant transcript; skipping post-bind live probes.
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  6 passed (6)
  ```
  ```text
  Test Files  9 passed (9)
       Tests  491 passed (491)
  ```
  ```text
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] current head 98d0decb1019672fef96f965253255bfa5254fe9
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] gateway websocket connected
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] calling sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="codex") with 1 image/png attachment (2987 bytes)
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] sessions_spawn returned {"status":"accepted","childSessionKey":"agent:codex:acp:6eab7e51-45ae-4352-8a71-cdcc8e9a2048","runId":"2fa403ae-f054-4412-8cff-1ee5150e18a2","mode":"run","note":"initial ACP task queued in isolated session; follow-ups continue in the bound thread."}
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] agent.wait returned {"runId":"2fa403ae-f054-4412-8cff-1ee5150e18a2","status":"ok","startedAt":1780029550433,"endedAt":1780029561941}
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] Codex image-analysis result: "cat"
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] PASS: sessions_spawn(runtime="acp") sent an image and Codex returned an image-analysis result
  ```
  ```json
  {
    "currentHead": "98d0decb1019672fef96f965253255bfa5254fe9",
    "runtime": "acp",
    "agentId": "codex",
    "tool": "sessions_spawn",
    "attachment": {
      "mimeType": "image/png",
      "bytes": 2987
    },
    "spawnPayload": {
      "status": "accepted",
      "childSessionKey": "agent:codex:acp:6eab7e51-45ae-4352-8a71-cdcc8e9a2048",
      "runId": "2fa403ae-f054-4412-8cff-1ee5150e18a2",
      "mode": "run"
    },
    "waitResult": {
      "runId": "2fa403ae-f054-4412-8cff-1ee5150e18a2",
      "status": "ok"
    },
    "assistantText": "cat"
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The current-head direct OpenClaw module path accepts an ACP image attachment, maps it to the Gateway base64 image source shape, and Gateway normalization keeps the image payload. The current-head live OpenClaw Gateway/Codex ACP run started the Gateway, connected over websocket, accepted `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` with a real `image/png` attachment, completed the ACP run successfully, and Codex returned the image-analysis result `cat`.
- **What was not tested:** The direct `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` proof covers the image attachment conversion/normalization and live Codex image classification path on this head. The separate ACP bind live test still skipped its post-bind image probe because that harness did not observe bound assistant transcript text, but the direct current-head proof above covers the requested live image-analysis behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit/integration tests plus local guard checks
- Target test file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`, `src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: ACP image attachments are accepted only through the gated ACP path, Gateway preserves the normalized image payload, non-ACP sessions and ACP-shaped sessions without persisted ACP metadata cannot bypass the normal image support check, the current main test typecheck accepts the Bonjour beacon assertion, and slower live ACP bind providers can be given a longer request timeout for proof runs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the ACP dispatch boundary and the Gateway bypass decision without adding external-service dependencies to CI, while the live timeout knob only affects manually enabled live proof runs.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: ACP image forwarding needed a Gateway attachment shape compatible with image normalization, and the Gateway bypass for ACP manual-spawn turns was keyed only on ACP-shaped session keys instead of confirmed ACP session state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression test for ACP-shaped session keys without ACP metadata, and the previous branch allowed unrelated shrinkwrap registry churn to obscure the functional change.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

  ```bash
    openclaw-codex-proof:jammy \
    sh -lc 'export PATH="/node24/bin:/usr/local/node/bin:$PATH"; node --import tsx /evidence/sessions-spawn-acp-image-live-proof-current.mjs 2>&1 | tee /evidence/sessions-spawn-acp-image-live-proof-current.log'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**

  ```text
  [sessions-spawn-acp-image-proof] agent.wait returned {"runId":"2fa403ae-f054-4412-8cff-1ee5150e18a2","status":"ok","startedAt":1780029550433,"endedAt":1780029561941}
  ```

  ```json
    },

    },
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The current-head direct OpenClaw module path accepts an ACP image attachment, maps it to the Gateway base64 image source shape, and Gateway normalization keeps the image payload. The current-head live OpenClaw Gateway/Codex ACP run started the Gateway, connected over websocket, accepted `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` with a real `image/png` attachment, completed the ACP run successfully, and Codex returned the image-analysis result `cat`.
- **What was not tested:** The direct `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` proof covers the image attachment conversion/normalization and live Codex image classification path on this head. The separate ACP bind live test still skipped its post-bind image probe because that harness did not observe bound assistant transcript text, but the direct current-head proof above covers the requested live image-analysis behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit/integration tests plus local guard checks
- Target test file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`, `src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: ACP image attachments are accepted only through the gated ACP path, Gateway preserves the normalized image payload, non-ACP sessions and ACP-shaped sessions without persisted ACP metadata cannot bypass the normal image support check, the current main test typecheck accepts the Bonjour beacon assertion, and slower live ACP bind providers can be given a longer request timeout for proof runs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the ACP dispatch boundary and the Gateway bypass decision without adding external-service dependencies to CI, while the live timeout knob only affects manually enabled live proof runs.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: ACP image forwarding needed a Gateway attachment shape compatible with image normalization, and the Gateway bypass for ACP manual-spawn turns was keyed only on ACP-shaped session keys instead of confirmed ACP session state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression test for ACP-shaped session keys without ACP metadata, and the previous branch allowed unrelated shrinkwrap registry churn to obscure the functional change.
